claim_boundary: Statistics are bounded by available evidence. Admission rates have wide CIs due to small cluster count per protocol (n=10–16). Dynamic SLAM neutral rate is based on 16 configurations across 5 sessions. No B0/B1/B2 per-baseline per-cluster admission flags exist → Fisher exact between baselines NOT computed. No claim of statistical significance made; CIs are reported for transparency.

admission_rate_statistics.method: bootstrap percentile 95% CI (10,000 resamples) + Wilson score 95% CI
admission_rate_statistics.observation: Admission rates range from 45.5% (same-day, n=11) to 56.2% (Hallway, n=16). All protocols' 95% CIs overlap substantially, consistent with a stable ~50% admission rate across settings. Hallway admission (56.2%) does not differ from pooled Aisle (17/35=48.6%, Fisher exact two-sided p=0.764) — Hallway validates rather than contradicts the Aisle admission regime.

label_purity_statistics.interpretation: Retained clusters have mean label purity >94%. High purity is a consequence of the admission criteria, not an independent finding.

session_coverage_statistics.interpretation: Retained clusters span 2–9 sessions per cluster across protocols.

admission_rate_statistics.limitation_fisher_baselines: FISHER EXACT BETWEEN B0/B1/B2 BASELINES NOT COMPUTED: Per-baseline per-cluster admission flags are not available in the current evidence. B0/B1/B2 comparisons exist as aggregate ladder-level admission rates from P100/P106 matrices, not as cluster-level binary (admitted/rejected) flags per baseline. Bootstrap CI on per-baseline rates could be computed IF per-baseline admission flags are added to retained/rejected CSV exports.

separation.method: Two groups fully separated at |ΔAPE|=0.006mm boundary
separation.note: No overlap between neutral and perturbed groups; no formal test needed for complete separation.

dynamic_slam_statistics.interpretation: Bootstrap 95% CI for neutral rate: [43.8%–87.5%]. Neutral group ΔAPE mean: 9.1e-05 mm (95% CI [-0.000818–0.001455]). Perturbed group ΔAPE mean: 3.5594 mm (95% CI [0.9208–6.198]). The neutral-perturbed boundary at |ΔAPE| ≤ 0.006 mm produces complete group separation (gap = 0.914 mm). With selective semantic frontend masks, DROID-SLAM trajectories remain neutral in 11/16 configurations. The neutral rate 95% CI includes 50%, cautioning against overclaiming: the evidence supports "mask selectivity is a necessary condition for trajectory neutrality" but not "selective masking always guarantees neutrality".

comparison_hallway_vs_aisle.interpretation: Hallway admission rate (56.2%) vs pooled Aisle (48.6%): Fisher exact p=0.7645. No evidence of difference between Hallway and Aisle admission regimes. Hallway serves as broader validation, not as a statistically distinct setting.

remaining_gaps: B0/B1/B2 per-baseline per-cluster admission flags not exported → Fisher exact between baselines not computed.
Dynamic SLAM: 16 configurations is a small sample for rate estimation. CI lower bound crosses 50%.

No statistical formalization for within-protocol cluster-level covariates (dynamic_ratio, session_count, label_purity as predictors) — would require logistic regression with n=10–16 clusters, underpowered.
Bootstrapping at the cluster level assumes clusters are exchangeable; session-level clustering is not modeled.
